# ATWS — Box-Jenkins-Treadway time series suite

> A four-part Python suite for univariate time series modelling following the
> Box-Jenkins-Treadway methodology, with an MCP server so an LLM can operate it.
> Components: fue (estimation + FUF forecasting), pyfug (graphics), art-tseries
> (model building + MCP). The method is iterative and decision-driven: the suite
> supplies evidence (graphs, tests, numbers); the agent/analyst supplies criterion.

## Operating the suite (read these first)

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): how an AI agent drives the suite — modes, non-negotiable
  rules (always present the model; anomalies are the analyst's decision; sequential
  construction from the previous optimum), and the .inp/.pre/.out/.fuf I/O conventions.
- [docs/TOOLS.md](docs/TOOLS.md): the 32 MCP tools, grouped by Box-Jenkins-Treadway stage.
- [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): suite architecture, the
  evidence-vs-criterion philosophy, guided vs autonomous, the policy/pipeline design.

## Install and connect

- `pip install atws` installs the whole suite + the `art-mcp` MCP server.
- `claude mcp add art -- art-mcp` connects it to Claude Code.

## Packages

- [README.md](README.md): ART overview, install, MCP usage, library usage.
- fue (PyPI): exact ML estimation engine + FUF forecasting (C engine + pure-Python fallback).
- pyfug (PyPI): high-definition graphics for time series analysis.

## Key concepts

- Guided mode: analyst decides at each node, agent advises. Autonomous mode:
  build_model decides and presents a final model (same engine, different "who confirms").
- Every estimation presents: the model equation (parameters + SE) and the residual
  diagnostics (residuals + ACF/PACF), titled with the model name.
- DCD = non-invertibility test (Davis-Chen-Dunsmuir); MEG = stochastic seasonality
  evaluation frequency by frequency, including the Nyquist; Shin-Fuller = formal
  non-stationarity test.
